finding: FP16 delivers 4x kernel speedup over FP32 with negligible power difference in burst mode (5 iterations)
interpretation: Burst FP16 appears efficient. However test-21 (60 iterations sustained) reveals FP16 draws 483W vs FP32 302W under sustained load — a 60% power increase not visible at 5 iterations.
significance: Demonstrates why short burst tests mislead energy analysis. Sustained testing is required for accurate CEI measurement.
